The above three formulas are all modified from *Bai Hu Tang*. *Bai Hu
Jia Ren Shen Tang* supplements qi and is indicated for mild cold
intolerance on the back and a superficial, large and hollow pulse. *Bai
Hu Jia Gui Zhi Tang* harmonizes the *Ying*-nutrients and unblocks
meridians. It is indicated for joint redness, swelling and pain due to
wind, dampness and heat obstructing meridians. *Bai Hu Jia Cang Zhu
Tang* clears heat and dries dampness. It is indicated for chest
discomfort and a red tongue with a white, greasy coating in damp-warm
syndrome (more heat than dampness).

***Zhu Ye Shi Gao Tang***

*Shang Han Lun* (*Treatise on Cold Damage*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Mon   | > 石膏 | *shí    | 30g | Gypsum Fibrosum                    |
| archs |        | gāo*    |     |                                    |
+=======+========+=========+=====+====================================+
|       | > 竹叶 | *zhú    | 9g  | Folium Phyllostachydis Henonis     |
|       |        | yè*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Mini  | > 人参 | *rén    | 6g  | Radix et Rhizoma Ginseng           |
| sters |        | shēn*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 麦冬 | *mài    | 12g | Radix Ophiopogonis                 |
|       |        | dōng*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Assi  | > 半夏 | *bàn    | 6g  | Rhizoma Pinelliae                  |
| stant |        | xià*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| E     | >      | *zhì    | 6g  | Radix et Rhizoma Glycyrrhizae      |
| nvoys | 炙甘草 | gān     |     | Praeparata cum Melle               |
|       |        | căo*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 粳米 | *jīng   | 9g  | Oryza Sativa L.                    |
|       |        | mĭ*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarchs: *Shi Gao* and *Zhu Ye* clear remaining heat in the qi phase.
*Zhu Ye* also relieves restlessness.

Ministers: *Ren Shen* strengthens the spleen and benefits qi. *Mai Dong*
nourishes yin and regenerates fluids.

Assistants: Although *Ban Xia* is warm and dry, it harmonizes the
stomach and bears qi down when used in combination with heat-clearing
and fluids-regenerating ingredients. In addition, it prevents stagnation
from tonic *Ren Shen* and *Mai Dong*.

Envoys: *Gan Cao* and *Jing Mi* supplement qi and nourish the stomach.

This formula is modified from *Bai Hu Tang* by removing *Zhi Mu* and
adding *Zhu Ye*, *Ren Shen*, *Ban Xia* and *Mai Dong*, and therefore
known as "a heat-clearing and tonic formula with extremely cold
ingredients" in *Yi Zong Jin Jian* (*Golden Mirror of the Medical
Tradition*).

**\[Method of administration\]** Decoct with water.

**\[Functions\]** Clears heat, regenerates fluids, supplements qi and
harmonizes the stomach.

**\[Indications\]** Damaged qi and fluids by unresolved heat in cold
damage, febrile disease and summer heat: Fever, profuse sweating,
restlessness, insomnia, shallow breathing, nausea, vomiting, a dry
mouth, a red tongue with a scanty coating, and a deficient, rapid pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a common formula for damaged qi
and fluids by unresolved heat in late stage febrile diseases. Retention
of unresolved heat in the qi phase may cause fever and sweating. Heat
disturbing the mind may cause restlessness and insomnia. Stomach yin
deficiency may affect descending of stomach qi and cause nausea and
vomiting. Heat damaging fluids may cause shallow breathing, a dry mouth,
a red tongue with a scanty coating and a deficient, rapid pulse. As a
result, the treatment principles are to clear heat, regenerate fluids,
benefit qi and harmonize the stomach.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Fever, profuse sweating, thirst,
restlessness, nausea, vomiting, a dry mouth, a dry and red tongue, and a
deficient, rapid pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For thirst and a foul breath due to exuberant stomach fire, add *Zhi
    Mu* (Rhizoma Anemarrhenae) and *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma Coptidis) to
    enhance the heat-clearing efficacy.

-   For ascending of deficiency fire due to stomach yin deficiency, add
    *Shi Hu* (Caulis Dendrobii), *Sha Shen* (Radix Adenophorae seu
    Glehniae) and *Tian Hua Fen* (Radix Trichosanthis) to clear heat and
    nourish yin.

3\. Modern use: Infantile fever in summer, sunstroke and fever in
late-stage pneumonia (heat damaging both qi and fluids). It can also be
used for excessive thirst in diabetes (stomach heat damaging yin).

### 4. Cautions & contraindications: This formula is contraindicated for patients with undamaged qi and fluids or with internal phlegm dampness.  {#cautions-contraindications-this-formula-is-contraindicated-for-patients-with-undamaged-qi-and-fluids-or-with-internal-phlegm-dampness. .unnumbered}

**4.2 Formulas that clear heat in heat in the *Ying*-nutrients phase and
cool blood**

Formulas that clear heat in the *Ying*-nutrients phase and cool blood
are indicated for heat entering the *Ying*-nutrients or blood phases.
Clinical manifestations of heat entering the *Ying*-nutrients phase may
include fever (especially at night), restlessness, insomnia, delirium,
and skin rashes. Clinical manifestations of heat entering the blood
phase may include fever, restlessness, mental confusion, delirium,
dark-purple skin rashes, hematemesis, and a deep-red, thorny tongue.
*Shui Niu* *Jiao* (Cornu Bubali) and *Sheng Di Huang* (Radix Rehmanniae)
are often used to clear heat in the *Ying*-nutrients phase and cool
blood. Pungent cool ingredients like *Jin Yin Hua* (Flos Lonicerae
Japonicae), *Lian Qiao* (Fructus Forsythiae) and *Zhu Ye* (Folium
Phyllostachydis Henonis) are often used for heat entering the
*Ying*-nutrients phase. Blood-circulating ingredients like *Dan Pi*
(Cortex Moutan) and *Chi Shao* (Radix Paeoniae Rubra) are often used for
heat entering the blood phase and prevention of blood stasis. Typical
formulas are *Qing Ying Tang* (Ying Level Clearing Decoction) and *Qing
Re Di Huang Tang* (Heat-Clearing Rehmannia Decoction).

***Qing Ying Tang***

*Wen Bing Tiao Bian* (*Systematic Differentiation of Warm Diseases*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
| Mo    | >       | *shuĭ   | 30g      | Cornu Bubali                |
| narch |  水牛角 | niú     |          |                             |
|       |         | jiăo*   |          |                             |
+=======+=========+=========+==========+=============================+
| Mini  | > 生地  | *shēng  | 15g      | Radix Rehmanniae            |
| sters |         | dì*     |          |                             |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
|       | > 玄参  | *xuán   | 9g       | Radix Scrophulariae         |
|       |         | shēn*   |          |                             |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
|       | > 麦冬  | *mài    | 9g       | Radix Ophiopogonis          |
|       |         | dōng*   |          |                             |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
| Assis | > 银花  | *yín    | 9g       | Flos Lonicerae Japonicae    |
| tants |         | huā*    |          |                             |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
|       | > 连翘  | *lián   | 9g       | Fructus Forsythiae          |
|       |         | qiào*   |          |                             |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
|       | > 竹叶  | *zhú    | 3g       | Folium Phyllostachydis      |
|       |         | yè*     |          | Henonis                     |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
|       | > 黄连  | *huáng  | 5g       | Rhizoma Coptidis            |
|       |         | lián*   |          |                             |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+
|       | > 丹参  | *dān    | 6g       | Radix et Rhizoma Salviae    |
|       |         | shēn*   |          | Miltiorrhizae               |
+-------+---------+---------+----------+-----------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarch: *Shui Niu* *Jiao* is salty and cold. It clears heat in the
*Ying*-nutrients and blood phases.

Ministers: *Sheng Di*, *Xuan Shen* and *Mai Dong* assist the efficacy of
*Shui Niu* *Jiao* in clearing heat and cooling blood, nourishing yin and
regenerating fluids.

Assistants: *Yin Hua*, *Lian Qiao* and *Zhu Ye* have three functions: 1)
to assist the monarch and ministers in clearing heat and removing
toxins; 2) to clear the remaining heat in the qi phase; 3) to dispel
heat out of the *Ying*-nutrients phase. *Huang Lian* is bitter and cold
and acts to clear the heart, purge fire, and remove toxins. *Dan Shen*
cools and circulates blood, resolves stasis and prevents blood
stagnation.

Compatibility characteristics of this formula: Clearing heat in the
*Ying*-nutrients phase, nourishing yin, harmonizing qi and
*Ying*-nutrients, and removing toxins.

**\[Method of administration\]** Decoct with water (note: decoct *Shui
Niu* *Jiao* first).

**\[Functions\]** Clears heat in the *Ying*-nutrients phase, remove
toxins, cools blood and resolves stasis.

**\[Indications\]** Heat entering the *Ying*-nutrients phase: Fever that
aggravates at night, restlessness, poor sleep, delirium, absence or
presence of thirst, skin rashes, a dry, deep-red tongue and a thready,
rapid pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a typical formula for early stage
of heat entering the *Ying*-nutrients phase. Heat consuming
*Ying*-nutrients' yin may cause fever that aggravates at night. Heat
disturbing the mind may cause restlessness, poor sleep and occasional
delirium. Unresolved heat in the qi phase may cause thirst. Heat
entering the *Ying*-nutrients may either cause mild thirst or cause
*Ying*-nutrients' yin to ascend and result in no thirst. Heat damaging
blood collaterals may cause skin rashes. A dry, deep-red tongue and a
thready, rapid pulse are signs indicative of heat entering the
*Ying*-nutrients' yin. As a result, the treatment strategies are to
clear the *Ying*-nutrients, remove toxins, and nourish yin.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Fever that is aggrevated at
night, restlessness, poor sleep, skin rashes, a dry, deep-red tongue and
a thready, rapid pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For excessive heat in the qi phase, add *Shi Gao* (Gypsum Fibrosum)
    and *Zhi Mu* (Rhizoma Anemarrhenae) to enhance the heat-clearing and
    fire-purging efficacy.

-   For mental confusion and delirium due to heat entering the
    pericardium, add *An Gong Niu Huang Wan* (Peaceful Palace Bovine
    Bezoar Pill) to clear the heart and open orifices.

-   For phlegm, add *Tian Zhu Huang* (Concretio Silicea Bambusae),
    *Chuan Bei Mu* (Bulbus Fritillariae Cirrhosae) and *Dan Nan Xing*
    (Arisaema cum Bile) to clear heat and resolve phlegm.

3\. Modern use: Epidemic encephalitis B, epidemic cerebrospinal
meningitis, sepsis, and enteric typhoid fever that can be diagnosed as
heat entering the *Ying*-nutrients phase.

4\. Cautions & contraindications: This formula is contraindicated for a
white, slippery tongue coating due to severe dampness, because it may
nourish yin and aggravate the dampness.

***Qing Re Di Huang Tang* (originally known as *Xi Jiao Di Huang
Tang*)**

*Wai Tai Mi Yao* (*Arcane Essentials from the Imperial Library*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-----------+---------+--------+-----+-------------------------------+
| Monarch   | >       | *shuĭ  | 30g | Cornu Bubali                  |
|           |  水牛角 | niú    |     |                               |
|           |         | jiăo*  |     |                               |
+===========+=========+========+=====+===============================+
| Minister  | > 生地  | *shēng | 15g | Radix Rehmanniae              |
|           |         | dì*    |     |                               |
+-----------+---------+--------+-----+-------------------------------+
| A         | > 赤芍  | *chì   | 12g | Radix Paeoniae Rubra          |
| ssistants |         | sháo*  |     |                               |
+-----------+---------+--------+-----+-------------------------------+
|           | >       | *mŭ    | 9g  | Cortex Moutan                 |
|           |  牡丹皮 | dān    |     |                               |
|           |         | pí*    |     |                               |
+-----------+---------+--------+-----+-------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarch: *Shui Niu* *Jiao* is bitter and cold. It cools blood, purges
fire and removes toxins.

Minister: *Sheng Di Huang* cools blood, nourishes yin and enhances the
heat-clearing and blood-cooling efficacy of *Shui Niu* *Jiao*.

Assistant: *Chi Shao* and *Dan Pi* clear heat, cool blood, stop bleeding
and, at the same time, circulate blood and resolve stasis.

This formula primarily clears heat, cools blood and removes toxins. In
addition, it stops bleeding and, at the same time, circulates blood and
resolves stasis. Just as Ye Tianshi explained, "Heat entering the blood
phase may consume blood or cause the blood to move recklessly, as a
result, it's essential to cool and circulate blood and prevent blood
stasis".

**\[Method of administration\]** Decoct with water (note: Decoct *Shui
Niu* *Jiao* first).

**\[Functions\]** Clears heat, removes toxins, cools blood and resolves
stasis.

**\[Indications\]** Heat entering the blood phase: Fever, restlessness,
mental confusion, delirium, dark-purple skin rashes, bleeding, a
deep-red or thorny tongue and a thready, rapid pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a typical formula for heat
entering the blood phase. Toxic heat in the blood may disturb the heart
and cause fever, restlessness, delirium and mental confusion. Heat
causes the blood to move recklessly and results in all kinds of
bleeding---hematemesis, nosebleed, bloody urine/stools or dark-purple
skin rashes. Blood consuming *Ying*-nutrients may cause a deep red or
thorny tongue and a thready, rapid pulse. As a result, the treatment
strategies are to clear heat, remove toxins, cool blood and resolve
stasis.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Fever, restlessness, dark-purple
skin rashes, a deep red tongue and a thready, rapid pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For dark-purple skin rashes, mental confusion and delirium, add *An
    Gong Niu Huang Wan* (Peaceful Palace Bovine Bezoar Pill) to clear
    heat, remove toxins and open orifices.

-   For bleeding in the upper body, add *Ce Bai Ye* (Cacumen
    Platycladi), *Bai Ji* (Rhizoma Bletillae), *Bai Mao Gen* (Rhizoma
    Imperatae) and *Qian Cao* (Radix et Rhizoma Rubiae).

-   For bleeding in the lower body (bloody urine), add *Xiao Ji* (Herba
    Cirsii) and *Bai Mao Gen* (Rhizoma Imperatae).

-   For bloody stools, add *Di Yu* (Radix Sanguisorbae) and *Huai Hua*
    (Flos Sophorae) to cool blood and stop bleeding.

3\. Modern use: Thrombocytopenic purpura, allergic purpura, severe
hepatitis, acute leukemia, sepsis, eruptive fever and ulcerative
bleeding that can be diagnosed as excessive heat in the blood phase.

### 4. Cautions & contraindications: Because ingredients in this formula clear heat and cool blood, it's contraindicated for patients with yang deficiency or qi deficiency.  {#cautions-contraindications-because-ingredients-in-this-formula-clear-heat-and-cool-blood-its-contraindicated-for-patients-with-yang-deficiency-or-qi-deficiency. .unnumbered}

**4.3 Formulas that clear heat and remove toxins**

Formulas that clear heat and remove toxins are indicated for toxic heat
in epidemic febrile disease. Clinical manifestations may include intense
fever, strong thirst with a desire to drink water, hematemesis, skin
rashes, delirium, coma, facial redness and swelling, chills, fever, sore
throat, incoherent speech, insomnia, a dry mouth and throat, and
deep-rooted boils/carbuncles. Heat-clearing and toxin-removing
ingredients like *Huang Qin* (Radix Scutellariae), *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma
Coptidis), *Lian Qiao* (Fructus Forsythiae) and *Jin Yin Hua* (Flos
Lonicerae Japonicae) are often used in combination with bitter, cold and
purgative ingredients like *Da Huang* (Radix et Rhizoma Rhei) and *Mang
Xiao* (Natrii Sulfas) as well as heat-clearing and urination-promoting
ingredients like *Zhi Zi* (Fructus Gardeniae) and *Zhu Ye* (Folium
Phyllostachydis Henonis). Typical formulas are *Huang Lian Jie Du Tang*
(Coptis Toxin-Resolving Decoction), *Liang Ge San* (Diaphragm-Cooling
Powder) and *Pu Ji Xiao Du Yin* (Universal Relief Toxin-Removing
Beverage).

***Huang Lian Jie Du Tang***

*Wai Tai Mi Yao* (*Arcane Essentials from the Imperial Library*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+
| Monarch   | > 黄连 | *huáng   | 9g  | Rhizoma Coptidis             |
|           |        | lián*    |     |                              |
+===========+========+==========+=====+==============================+
| Ministers | > 黄芩 | *huáng   | 6g  | Radix Scutellariae           |
|           |        | qín*     |     |                              |
+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+
|           | > 黄柏 | *huáng   | 6g  | Cortex Phellodendri          |
|           |        | băi*     |     | Chinensis                    |
+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+
| Assistant | > 栀子 | *zhī zĭ* | 6g  | Fructus Gardeniae            |
+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarch: *Huang Lian* clears fire in the heart and middle *jiao*.

Ministers: *Huang Qin* clears fire in the upper *jiao* and *Huang Bai*
clears fire in the lower *jiao*.

Assistant: clears fire in all three *jiao* and purges heat through
promoting urination.

This formula consists of bitter and cold ingredients to reduce fire and
remove toxins. It is of paramount importance to seek the root cause in
clinical treatment.

**\[Method of administration\]** Decoct with water.

**\[Functions\]** Reduces fire and removes toxins.

**\[Indications\]** Excessive toxic fire/heat in all three *jiao*:
Intense fever, restlessness, a dry mouth and throat, dark yellow urine,
incoherent speech, insomnia, hematemesis, nosebleed, skin rashes,
diarrhea, jaundice (due to damp heat), deep-rooted boils/carbuncles, a
red tongue with a yellow coating and a rapid and forceful pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a common formula for excessive
toxic fire/heat in all three *jiao*. Exuberant toxic fire scorching body
fluids may cause intense fever, a dry mouth and throat and dark, yellow
urine. Toxic fire disturbing the mind may cause restlessness and
incoherent speech. Toxic fire may cause the blood to move recklessly and
result in hematemesis, nosebleed and skin rashes. Downward flow of toxic
fire to the large intestine may cause diarrhea. Toxic fire may affect
the muscles and cause the qi and blood to stagnate, resulting in
deep-rooted boils/carbuncles. As a result, the treatment strategies are
to reduce fire and remove toxins.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Intense fever, restlessness, a
dry mouth and throat, a red tongue with a yellow coating and a rapid,
forceful pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For hematemesis, nosebleed or skin rashes, add *Sheng Di* (Radix
    Rehmanniae), *Dan Pi* (Cortex Moutan), *Zi Cao* (Radix Arnebiae),
    *Bai Mao Gen* (Rhizoma Imperatae) and *Ce Bai Ye* (Cacumen
    Platycladi) to cool blood and stop bleeding.

-   For jaundice due to damp heat, add *Yin Chen* (Herba Artemisiae
    Scopariae) and *Da Huang* (Radix et Rhizoma Rhei) to clear heat and
    resolve dampness.

-   For red, swollen and painful deep-rooted boils/carbuncles, add *Pu
    Gong Yin* (Herba Taraxaci), *Yin Hua* (Flos Lonicerae Japonicae) and
    *Lian Qiao* (Fructus Forsythiae) to enhance the efficacy of removing
    toxins and eliminating boils/carbuncles.

3\. Modern use: Sepsis, pyosepticemia, acute bacillary dysentery, acute
enteritis, acute icteric hepatitis, acute urinary tract infection,
epidemic cerebrospinal meningitis and epidemic encephalitis B that can
be diagnosed as excessive toxic fire/heat.

### 4. Cautions & contraindications: Because ingredients in this formula are extremely bitter and cold, overuse or use over a long period of time may damage the spleen and stomach. It is indicated for exuberant fire only.  {#cautions-contraindications-because-ingredients-in-this-formula-are-extremely-bitter-and-cold-overuse-or-use-over-a-long-period-of-time-may-damage-the-spleen-and-stomach.-it-is-indicated-for-exuberant-fire-only. .unnumbered}

**\[Annex formula\]** *Xie Xin Tang* (Heart-Draining Decoction) from the
*Jin Gui Yao* *Lüè* (*Essentials from the Golden Cabinet*)

\[Ingredients\] *Da Huang* (Radix et Rhizoma Rhei), *Huang Lian*
(Rhizoma Coptidis) and *Huang Qin* (Radix Scutellariae)

\[Actions\] Clears heat, purges fire and stops bleeding.

\[Indications\] Exuberant internal fire causes blood to move recklessly:
Hematemesis, nosebleed, restlessness, thirst, dark yellow urine,
incomplete bowel evacuation or constipation, a red tongue with a thin,
yellow coating and a rapid pulse.

Both *Xie Xin Tang* and *Huang Lian Jie Du Tang* clear heat and remove
toxins. *Xie Xin Tang* is indicated for bleeding in the upper body. It
uses *Da Huang* to purge heat through promoting bowel movements. *Huang
Lian Jie Du Tang* is indicated for high-grade fever, bleeding, diarrhea,
jaundice and deep-rooted boils/carbuncles due to toxic heat. It uses
*Zhi Zi* to purge heat through promoting urination.

***Liang Ge San***

*Tai Ping Hui Min He Ji Ju Fang* (*Beneficial Formulas from the Taiping
Imperial Pharmacy*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Mo    | > 连翘 | *lián   | 15g | Fructus Forsythiae                 |
| narch |        | qiào*   |     |                                    |
+=======+========+=========+=====+====================================+
| Mini  | > 黄芩 | *huáng  | 6g  | Radix Scutellariae                 |
| sters |        | qín*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 栀子 | *zhī    | 6g  | Fructus Gardeniae                  |
|       |        | zĭ*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Assis | > 薄荷 | *bò hé* | 6g  | Herba Menthae                      |
| tants |        |         |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 大黄 | *dà     | 9g  | Radix et Rhizoma Rhei              |
|       |        | huáng*  |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 芒硝 | *máng   | 9g  | Natrii Sulfas                      |
|       |        | xiāo*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Envoy | >      | *zhì    | 9g  | Radix et Rhizoma Glycyrrhizae      |
|       | 炙甘草 | gān     |     | Praeparata cum Melle               |
|       |        | căo*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarch: *Lian Qiao* clears heat and removes toxins.

Ministers: *Huang Qin* clears heat in the chest and diaphragm. *Zhi Zi*
purges fire in three *jiao* and promotes urination.

Assistants: *Bo He* and *Zhu Ye* assist to clear heat. *Da Huang* and
*Mang Xiao* purge fire, promote bowel movements, and combine with *Zhi
Zi* to induce heat to move downward.

Envoy: *Gan Cao* and honey moderate the purgative efficacy of *Mang
Xiao* and *Da Huang* and coordinate the other ingredients.

The formula clears heat in the upper body and at the same time, promotes
bowel movements.

**\[Method of administration\]** Add 3g of *Zhu Ye*, 3g of honey and
decoct with water.

**\[Functions\]** Purges fire and promotes bowel movements.

**\[Indications\]** Exuberant fire heat in the upper and middle *jiao*:
Fever, thirst, flushed face, chapped lips, mouth or tongue ulceration,
sore throat, hematemesis, constipation, dark yellow urine, incomplete
bowel evacuation, a red tongue with a yellow coating and a slippery,
rapid pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a common formula for exuberant
fire in the upper and middle *jiao*. Heat accumulating the upper *jiao*
may scorch body fluids and result in fever, thirst and feverish
sensations in the chest and diaphragm. Ascending of fire may cause
flushed face, chapped lips, mouth or tongue ulceration, sore throat and
hematemesis. Dry heat in the middle *jiao* may cause constipation and
dark, yellow urine. As a result, the treatment strategies are to clear
heat, purge fire and promote bowel movements.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Feverish sensations in the chest
and diaphragm, flushed face, chapped lips, restlessness, thirst, a red
tongue with a yellow coating and a rapid pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For severe feverish sensations and a strong thirst, add *Shi Gao*
    (Gypsum Fibrosum) and *Tian Hua Fen* (Radix Trichosanthis) to clear
    heat and generate fluids.

-   For red, swollen and sore throat, add *Xuan Shen* (Radix
    Scrophulariae), *She Gan* (Rhizoma Belamcandae) and *Jie Geng*
    (Radix Platycodonis) to remove toxins and benefit the throat.

-   For severe mouth or tongue ulcerations, add *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma
    Coptidis) to clear heart fire.

3\. Modern use: Pharyngitis, stomatitis, acute tonsillitis, biliary
tract infection and acute icteric hepatitis that can be diagnosed as
fire heat in the upper and middle *jiao*.

### 4. Cautions & contraindications: For pregnant women or patients with spleen/stomach weakness, decrease the doses of *Da Huang* and *Mang Xiao* or decrease their doses.  {#cautions-contraindications-for-pregnant-women-or-patients-with-spleenstomach-weakness-decrease-the-doses-of-da-huang-and-mang-xiao-or-decrease-their-doses. .unnumbered}

***Pu Ji Xiao Du Yin***

*Dong Yuan Shi Xiao Fang* (*Li Dong-yuan's Proven Formulas*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Mon   | > 黄连 | *huáng  | *   | Rhizoma Coptidis                   |
| archs |        | lián*   | *15 |                                    |
|       |        |         | g** |                                    |
+=======+========+=========+=====+====================================+
|       | > 黄芩 | *huáng  | 15g | Radix Scutellariae                 |
|       |        | qín*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Mini  | >      | *niú    | 3g  | Fructus Arctii                     |
| sters | 牛蒡子 | bàng    |     |                                    |
|       |        | zĭ*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 连翘 | *lián   | 3g  | Fructus Forsythiae                 |
|       |        | qiào*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 薄荷 | *bò hé* | 3g  | Herba Menthae                      |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 僵蚕 | *jiāng  | 2g  | Bombyx Batryticatus                |
|       |        | cán*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Assis | > 玄参 | *xuán   | 6g  | Radix Scrophulariae                |
| tants |        | shēn*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 马勃 | *mă bó* | 3g  | Lasiosphaera seu Calvatia          |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | >      | *băn    | 3g  | Radix Isatidis                     |
|       | 板蓝根 | lán     |     |                                    |
|       |        | gēn*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 桔梗 | *jié    | 6g  | Radix Platycodonis                 |
|       |        | gĕng*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 陈皮 | *chén   | 6g  | Pericarpium Citri Reticulatae      |
|       |        | pí*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 升麻 | *shēng  | 2g  | Rhizoma Cimicifugae                |
|       |        | má*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 柴胡 | *chái   | 6g  | Radix Bupleuri                     |
|       |        | hú*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Envoy | > 甘草 | *gān    | 6g  | Radix et Rhizoma Glycyrrhizae      |
|       |        | căo*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarchs: *Huang Lian* and *Huang Qin* clear toxic heat and purge fire
in the upper *jiao*.

Ministers: *Niu Bang Zi*, *Lian Qiao*, *Bo He* and *Jiang Can* dispel
wind heat in the upper *jiao*.

Assistants: *Xuan She*n, *Ma Bo*, and *Ban Lan Gen* enhance the effect
of monarchs in clearing toxic heat. *Gan Cao*, Jie Geng and Bo Ho
benefit the throat; *Xuan Shen* nourishes yin, regenerates fluids and
stops thirst; *Chen Pi* regulates qi and relieves swelling. *Sheng Ma*
and *Chai Hu* dispel wind heat and guide the other ingredients to ascend
to the head/face.

Envoy: *Gan Cao* coordinates the other ingredients.

**\[Method of administration\]** Buccal honey pills or decoct with
water.

**\[Functions\]** Clears heat, removes toxins, and dispels wind.

**\[Indications\]** Fever with a swollen head: Chills, fever, head/face
redness, swelling and burning sensation, red and swollen eyes, a red,
sore and swollen throat, a dry mouth, thirst, a red tongue with a yellow
coating and a superficial, rapid and forceful pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a common formula for fever with a
swollen head. Pestilence wind heat affecting the exterior may cause
chills and fever. Ascending of toxic heat from qi and blood stagnation
may cause head/face redness, a swelling and burning sensation, and a
red, swollen and sore throat. Heat consuming body fluids may cause a dry
mouth and thirst. As a result, the treatment strategies are to clear
heat, remove toxins and dispel wind.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Head/face redness, swelling and
burning sensation, chills, fever, sore throat, a red tongue with a
yellow coating and a superficial, rapid pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For constipation, add *Da Huang* (Radix et Rhizoma Rhei) to clear
    heat and promote bowel movements.

-   For testicle pain, add *Chuan Lian Zi* (Fructus Toosendan) and *Long
    Dan Cao* (Radix et Rhizoma Gentianae) to clear heat in the liver
    meridian.

3\. Modern use: Facial erysipelas, epidemic parotitis, acute
tonsillitis, acute lymphadenitis and acute suppurative otitis media that
can be diagnosed as pestilence wind heat.

### 4. Cautions & contraindications: Because ingredients in this formula are bitter, cold, pungent and dispersing, patients with constitutional yin deficiency or spleen deficiency should use this formula with caution. {#cautions-contraindications-because-ingredients-in-this-formula-are-bitter-cold-pungent-and-dispersing-patients-with-constitutional-yin-deficiency-or-spleen-deficiency-should-use-this-formula-with-caution. .unnumbered}

**4.4 Formulas that clear heat in qi and blood phases**

Formulas that clear heat in qi and blood phases are indicated for severe
fire in the qi and blood phases. Common heat/fire-clearing ingredients
like *Shi Gao* (Gypsum Fibrosum), *Zhi Mu* (Rhizoma Anemarrhenae) and
*Zhu Ye* (Folium Phyllostachydis Henonis) are often used in combination
with blood-cooling ingredients like *Shui Niu* *Jiao* (Cornu Bubali),
*Sheng Di* (Radix Rehmanniae), *Chi Shao* (Radix Paeoniae Rubra) and
*Dan Pi* (Cortex Moutan) as well as heat-clearing and toxin-removing
ingredients like *Huang Qin* (Radix Scutellariae), *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma
Coptidis) and *Lian Qiao* (Fructus Forsythiae). A typical formula is
*Qing Wen Bai Du Yin* (Epidemic-Clearing Toxin-Resolving Beverage).

***Qing Wen Bai Du Yin***

*Yi Zhen Yi De* (*Achievements in the Treatment of Epidemic Rashes*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 石膏  | *shí     | 24-240g    | Gypsum Fibrosum                  |
|         | gāo*     |            |                                  |
+=========+==========+============+==================================+
| > 知母  | *zhī mŭ* | 9g         | Rhizoma Anemarrhenae             |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 甘草  | *gān     | 9g         | Radix et Rhizoma Glycyrrhizae    |
|         | căo*     |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 竹叶  | *zhú yè* | 9g         | Folium Phyllostachydis Henonis   |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 黄连  | *huáng   | 3-18g      | Rhizoma Coptidis                 |
|         | lián*    |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 黄芩  | *huáng   | 9g         | Radix Scutellariae               |
|         | qín*     |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 栀子  | *zhī zĭ* | 9g         | Fructus Gardeniae                |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 连翘  | *lián    | 9g         | Fructus Forsythiae               |
|         | qiào*    |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| >       | *shuĭ    | 60-240g    | Cornu Bubali                     |
|  水牛角 | niú      |            |                                  |
|         | jiăo*    |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 生地  | *shēng   | 6-30g      | Radix Rehmanniae                 |
|         | dì*      |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 赤芍  | *chì     | 9g         | Radix Paeoniae Rubra             |
|         | sháo*    |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 玄参  | *xuán    | 9g         | Radix Scrophulariae              |
|         | shēn*    |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 丹皮  | *mŭ dān  | 9g         | Cortex Moutan                    |
|         | pí*      |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+
| > 桔梗  | *jié     | 9g         | Radix Platycodonis               |
|         | gĕng*    |            |                                  |
+---------+----------+------------+----------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

This formula is modified form *Bai Hu Tang*, *Qing Re Di Huang Tang* and
*Huang Lian Jie Du Tang*. *Shi Gao, Zhi Mu, Gan Cao* and *Zhu Ye*
(modified *Bai Hu Tang*) clear heat in the qi phase and generate fluids.
*Huang Lian*, *Huang Qin*, *Zhi Zi* and *Lian Qiao* (modified *Huang
Lian Jie Du Tang*) purge fire and remove toxic heat. *Shui Niu* *Jiao*,
*Sheng Di*, *Chi Shao*, *Dan Pi*, and *Xuan Shen* (modified *Qing Re Di
Huang Tang*) remove toxic heat in the blood phase, nourish yin, cool
blood and resolve stasis. Since toxic heat affects the upper *jiao*,
*Jie Geng* is used to induce the other ingredients to ascend.

**\[Method of administration\]** Decoct with water.

**\[Functions\]** Clears heat, removes toxins, cools blood and purges
fire.

**\[Indications\]** Severe fire in the qi and blood phases due to toxic
heat in epidemic febrile diseases: Intense fever, strong thirst, a
splitting headache, severe restlessness, delirium, coma, skin rashes,
hematemesis, nosebleed, convulsions, extreme limb coldness, a deep red
tongue and chapped lips. The pulse is deep, thready and rapid/deep and
rapid/superficial, large and rapid.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a typical formula for severe fire
in the qi and blood phases due to toxic heat in epidemic febrile
disease. Extreme heat in the qi phase may cause intense fever and strong
thirst. Toxic heat disturbing the mind may cause splitting headache,
restlessness, delirium and coma. Exuberant heat in the blood phase may
cause the blood to move recklessly and result in skin rashes,
hematemesis and nosebleed. Internal toxic heat may stir liver wind and
cause convulsions. The more seriously the heat enteres the four limbs,
in the more pronounced the fake appearance of cold will be. A deep,
thready and rapid pulse indicates toxic heat entering the deeper area. A
deep and rapid pulse indicates moderate toxic heat. A superficial, large
and rapid pulse indicates mild toxic heat. Severe fire in the qi and
blood phases often occurs as a result of critical conditions. As a
result, the treatment strategies are to clear heat, remove toxins, cool
blood and purge fire.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Intense fever, strong thirst, a
splitting headache, restlessness, delirium, coma, skin rashes,
hematemesis, nosebleed, a deep red tongue and chapped lips and a deep,
rapid pulse.

2\. Modifications

It's necessary to modify the doses of *Shi Gao*, *Shui Niu* *Jiao*,
*Sheng Di* and *Huang Lian* according to the predominance of heat in the
qi phase, heat in the blood phase or severity of toxic heat (a big dose
for intense heat and a smaller dose for mild heat).

-   For delirium and coma, combine with *An Gong Niu Huang Wan*.

-   For convulsions due to heat stirring wind, add *Ling Yang* *Jiao*
    (Cornu Saigae Tataricae), *Gou Teng* (Ramulus Uncariae Cum Uncis)
    and *Di Long* (Pheretima) to remove wind and stop convulsions.

3\. Modern use: Epidemic encephalitis B, epidemic cerebrospinal
meningitis, sepsis, pyosepticemia, severe hepatitis and epidemic
hemorrhagic fever that can be diagnosed as severe fire in the qi and
blood phases.

4\. Cautions & contraindications: Because ingredients in this formula
are cool and cold, patients with spleen/stomach weakness should use this
formula with caution.

**4.5 Formulas that clear heat in zang-fu organs**

Formulas that clear heat in zang-fu organs are indicated for exuberant
fire heat involving zang-fu organs, such as fire heat in the heart
meridian, damp heat in the liver and gallbladder, accumulated heat in
the lung, hidden fire in the spleen and stomach and accumulated heat in
the intestines. Common heat-clearing ingredients include *Huang Lian*
(Rhizoma Coptidis), *Zhi Zi* (Fructus Gardeniae) and *Mu Tong* (Caulis
Akebiae). Common liver-clearing ingredients include *Long Dan Cao*
(Radix et Rhizoma Gentianae), *Xia Ku Ca*o (Spica Prunellae) and *Qing
Dai* (Indigo Naturalis). Common lung-clearing ingredients include *Huang
Qin* (Radix Scutellariae), *Sang Bai Pi* (Cortex Mori), *Shi Gao*
(Gypsum Fibrosum) and *Zhi Mu* (Rhizoma Anemarrhenae). Common
stomach-clearing ingredients include *Shi Gao* (Gypsum Fibrosum) and
*Huang Lian* (Rhizoma Coptidis). Common intestine-clearing ingredients
include *Bai Tou Weng* (Radix Pulsatillae), *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma
Coptidis) and *Huang Bai* (Cortex Phellodendri Chinensis). Typical
formulas are *Dao Chi San* (Red Guiding Powder), *Long Dan Xie Gan Tang*
(Gentian Liver-Draining Decoction), *Ge Gen Huang Qin Huang Lian Tang*
(Pueraria, Scutellaria, and Coptis Decoction), *Zuo Jin Wan* (Left Metal
Pill), *Xie Bai San* (White-Draining Powder), *Qing Wei San*
(Stomach-Clearing Powder), *Yu Nǚ Jian* (Jade Lady Decoction), *Shao Yao
Tang* (Peony Decoction) and *Bai Tou Weng Tang* (Pulsatilla Decoction).

***Dao Chi San***

*Xiao Er Yao Zheng Zhi Jue* (*Key to Diagnosis and Treatment of
Children's Diseases*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+
| Monarch   | > 木通 | *mù      | 6g  | Caulis Akebiae               |
|           |        | tōng*    |     |                              |
+===========+========+==========+=====+==============================+
| Minister  | > 生地 | *shēng   | 6g  | Radix Rehmanniae             |
|           |        | dì*      |     |                              |
+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+
| Assist    | >      | *gān căo | 6g  | Radix Tenuis Glycyrrhizae    |
| ant/envoy | 甘草梢 | shāo*    |     |                              |
+-----------+--------+----------+-----+------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarch: *Mu Tong* enters the heart and small intestine meridians. It
clears fire in the heart meridian and reduces heat in the small
intestine meridian.

Minister: *Sheng Di* enters the heart and kidney meridians. It clears
heat and nourishes yin.

Assistant: *Zhu Ye* (Folium Phyllostachydis Henonis) is combined (when
decocting) to relieve restlessness and promote urination. *Gan Cao Shao*
clears heat, removes toxins, and coordinates the other ingredients.

This formula reduces heat, nourishes yin without retaining pathogenic
factors and promotes urination without damaging yin.

**\[Method of administration\]** Add 3g of *Zhu Ye* and decoct with
water.

**\[Functions\]** Clears the heart, promotes urination and nourishes
yin.

**\[Indications\]** Fire/heat in the heart meridian: Feverish sensations
in the chest, a red face, thirst with a desire to drink cold water,
mouth or tongue ulceration, a red tongue and a rapid pulse.
Alternatively, painful urination and dark yellow urine may be present
due to downward flow of heat to the small intestine.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a common formula for fire heat in
the heart meridian. Heat in the heart meridian may disturb the heart and
chest and cause feverish sensations. Ascending of heart fire may cause a
red face and mouth or tongue ulceration. Fire heat damaging yin may
cause thirst with a desire to drink cold water. The heart meridian is
external-internally connected with the small intestine meridian.
Downward flow of heat to the small intestine may cause painful urination
and dark yellow urine. As a result, the treatment strategies are to
clear the heart, nourish yin and promote urination.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Restlessness, feverish sensation
in the chest, mouth or tongue ulceration, a red tongue and a rapid
pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For severe heart fire, add *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma Coptidis) to clear
    heart fire.

-   For painful urination or dysuria, add *Che Qian Zi* (Semen
    Plantaginis), *Bian Xu* (Herba Polygoni Avicularis), *Qu Mai* (Herba
    Dianthi) and *Hua Shi* (Talcum) to enhance the efficacy of clearing
    heat and promoting urination.

-   For severe yin deficiency, add *Mai Dong* (Radix Ophiopogonis) to
    clear the heart and nourish yin.

3\. Modern use: Stomatitis, thrush and infantile night crying that can
be diagnosed as fire heat in the heart meridian. Its modifications can
be used to treat acute urinary tract infection due to damp heat in the
lower *jiao*.

## 4. Cautions & contraindications: Because of the cold property of some ingredients (*Mu Tong* and *Sheng Di*), patients with deficiency cold in the spleen and stomach should use this formula with caution.  {#cautions-contraindications-because-of-the-cold-property-of-some-ingredients-mu-tong-and-sheng-di-patients-with-deficiency-cold-in-the-spleen-and-stomach-should-use-this-formula-with-caution. .unnumbered}

***Long Dan Xie Gan Tang***

*Yi Fang Ji Jie* (*Medical Formulas Collected and Analyzed*)

**\[Ingredients\]**

+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Mo    | >      | *lóng   | 6g  | Radix et Rhizoma Gentianae         |
| narch | 龙胆草 | dăn     |     |                                    |
|       |        | căo*    |     |                                    |
+=======+========+=========+=====+====================================+
| Mini  | > 黄芩 | *huáng  | 9g  | Radix Scutellariae                 |
| sters |        | qín*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 栀子 | *zhī    | 9g  | Fructus Gardeniae                  |
|       |        | zĭ*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Assis | > 泽泻 | *zé     | 6g  | Rhizoma Alismatis                  |
| tants |        | xiè*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 木通 | *mù     | 6g  | Caulis Akebiae                     |
|       |        | tōng*   |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | >      | *chē    | 9g  | Semen Plantaginis                  |
|       | 车前子 | qián    |     |                                    |
|       |        | zĭ*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 当归 | *dāng   | 6g  | Radix Angelicae Sinensis           |
|       |        | guī*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 生地 | *shēng  | 9g  | Radix Rehmanniae                   |
|       |        | dì*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
|       | > 柴胡 | *chái   | 6g  | Radix Bupleuri                     |
|       |        | hú*     |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+
| Envoy | > 甘草 | *gān    | 6g  | Radix et Rhizoma Glycyrrhizae      |
|       |        | căo*    |     |                                    |
+-------+--------+---------+-----+------------------------------------+

**\[Explanations of the formula\]**

Monarch: *Long Dan Cao* clear excess fire and resolve damp heat in the
liver and gallbladder.

Ministers: *Huang Qin* and *Zhi Zi* clear heat, reduce fire, dry
dampness, and enhance the efficacy of *Long Dan Cao* in clearing fire
and resolving damp heat in the liver and gallbladder.

Assistants: *Ze Xie*, *Mu Tong* and *Che Qian Zi* clear heat and resolve
dampness through urination. *Dang Gui* and *Sheng Di* nourish yin and
blood. *Chai Hu* soothes the qi of the liver and gallbladder and induces
the other ingredients to the liver/gallbladder meridians.

Envoy: *Gan Cao* clears heat, coordinates the other ingredients,
protects the stomach and harmonizes the middle *jiao*.

**\[Method of administration\]** Take 6-9g honeyed pills or 3-6g water
pills for each dose, 2 doses a day. Alternatively, decoct with water.

**\[Functions\]** Clears excess fire in the liver and gallbladder and
resolves damp heat in the lower *jiao*.

**\[Indications\]**

-   Ascending of excess fire in the liver and gallbladder: Pain in the
    hypochondriac region, a headache, red eyes, a bitter mouth,
    deafness, ear swelling, a red tongue with a yellow coating and a
    wiry, rapid and forceful pulse.

-   Downward flow of damp heat in the liver and gallbladder: Scrotal
    swelling, itchy scrotum, scrotal sweating, turbid urine, yellow,
    smelly vaginal discharge, a red tongue with a yellow, greasy coating
    and a wiry, rapid and forceful pulse.

**\[Diagnosis & treatment\]** This is a common formula for excess fire
in the liver and gallbladder and downward flow of damp heat to the lower
*jiao*. Ascending of liver/gallbladder fire along the pathway of
meridians may cause headache, red eyes, a bitter mouth, deafness and ear
swelling. Liver qi stagnation may cause pain in the hypochondriac
region. Downward flow of damp heat in the liver and gallbladder to the
lower *jiao* may cause itchy scrotum, scrotal swelling, scrotal sweating
and yellow, smelly vaginal discharge. As a result, the treatment
strategies are to clear excess fire in the liver and gallbladder and
resolve damp heat in the lower *jiao*.

**\[Clinical use\]**

1\. Characteristic signs and symptoms: Pain in the hypochondriac region,
a headache, red eyes, a bitter mouth, turbid urine, scrotal swelling,
itchy scrotum, scrotal sweating, yellow, smelly vaginal discharge, a red
tongue with a yellow or yellow, greasy coating and a wiry, rapid and
forceful pulse.

2\. Modifications

-   For exuberant excess fire, remove *Mu Tong* and C*he Qian Zi* and
    add *Huang Lian* (Rhizoma Coptidis) to enhance the fire-reducing
    efficacy.

-   For more dampness than heat, remove *Huang Qin* and *Sheng Di* and
    add *Hua Shi* (Talcum) and *Yi Yi Ren* (Semen Coicis) to enhance the
    dampness-resolving efficacy.

-   For a severe headache and red eyes, add *Ju Hua* (Flos
    Chrysanthemi), *Sang Ye* (Folium Mori) and *Xia Ku Cao* (Spica
    Prunellae) to clear the liver and benefit the eyes.

-   For genital ulcer and scrotal swelling, pain and redness, remove
    *Chai Hu* and add *Lian Qiao* (Fructus Forsythiae), *Huang Bai*
    (Cortex Phellodendri Chinensis) and *Chi Xiao Dou* (Semen Phaseoli)
    to reduce fire and remove toxins.

3\. Modern use: Refractory chronic migraine, hypertension, acute
conjunctivitis, iridocyclitis, furuncle of the external auditory meatus,
rhinitis, acute icteric hepatitis, acute cholecystitis, acute
nephropyelitis, acute cystitis, urethritis, vulvitis, testitis, inguinal
adenitis, acute pelvic inflammation and herpes zoster that can be
diagnosed as excess fire or damp heat in the liver meridian.

### 4. Cautions & contraindications: Because most ingredients in this formula are bitter and cold and may damage the spleen and stomach, patients should discontinue immediately when their symptoms are alleviated. Patients with spleen/stomach weakness should use this formula with caution.  {#cautions-contraindications-because-most-ingredients-in-this-formula-are-bitter-and-cold-and-may-damage-the-spleen-and-stomach-patients-should-discontinue-immediately-when-their-symptoms-are-alleviated.-patients-with-spleenstomach-weakness-should-use-this-formula-with-caution. .unnumbered}

| ng (*Xiao E | Xiong, Zhi Zi,  | l    | pain, restlessness,           |

|  Zhi Jue*)  | Huo, Fang Feng, | fire | lie flat, dark yellow urine,  |

| -wang-xiao- |                 |      | excessive pulse, infantile    |

| ng Hui Wan  | Zi, Huang Lian, | l    | blurred vision, restlessness, |
| (*Huang Di  | Huang Bai,      | iver | delirium, mania, constipation |
| Su Wen Xuan | Huang Qin, Lu   | fire | or dark yellow urine with     |
|  Ming Fang  | Hui, Qing Dai,  |      | hesitant urination            |

| g-gui-long- | Xiang and She   |      |                               |

| ng-di-su-we |                 |      |                               |
